title: stelis coracina
intro: Stelis coracina, also known as the Antillyan orchid, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This orchid has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Stelis coracina are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Stelis coracina typically involve boiling the plant's roots and leaves in water to create a medicinal tea, which is often used to treat a range of health conditions.
